screens.name: Song Score Detail
screens.purpose: Present compact lead-sheet chord symbols in the user-selected Scheherazade treatment.

ux_rules: Use the bundled Scheherazade New Bold face for root letters, triangle and hyphen qualities, extension numbers, slash marks, bass letters, and chord accidentals.
Match the approved Scheherazade row from the recorded neutral whole-font comparison instead of exploring another font.
Keep major and minor quality suffixes at 72% on the native baseline.
Keep root and slash-bass accidentals inline with their note names in the same Scheherazade face.
Raise only true alterations and modifiers such as flat-nine and sharp-nine.
Preserve stored chord strings, alphaTex generation, staff engraving, responsive score density, loading behavior, and error behavior.

screen_contexts.layout_intent: Roll back the rejected Edwin implementation and reproduce the exact Scheherazade single-font option selected by the user.
interaction_risks: Retaining Edwin in any alphanumeric chord part would violate the explicit rollback.
Keeping the compact Edwin accidental segmentation would not match the selected Scheherazade comparison row.
Raising triangle-seven or minus-seven would violate the previously approved notation hierarchy.
Returning suffixes to full root size could recreate mobile crowding.
notes: The user-selected direction is already browser-recorded as the Scheherazade row in evidence/coherence-neutral-whole-font-grid.png.
The rejected Edwin mobile baseline is recorded in evidence/scheherazade-rollback-0-edwin.png.
No further typeface comparison is required because the user made the final selection.

constraints: Set the document chord font back to the existing Score Scheherazade New family.
Remove the Score Edwin @font-face and Edwin-Bold document-font loading path.
Render root and slash-bass accidentals inline with their note names through typographic accidental conversion.
Keep triangle, hyphen, and extension quality text at 72% with no baseline shift.
Keep the established raised modifier scale and baseline shift for flat-nine, sharp-nine, sharp-five, and equivalent supported modifiers.
Preserve the original source chord in data-source-chord and keep the SVG transformation idempotent.
Verify 375px, 390px, and 430px at devicePixelRatio 3 plus a 1024px desktop viewport.
Do not change song data, alphaTex generation, score density, staff SMuFL settings, global theme, dependencies, or routes.

critical_judgments: Does the rendered score match the selected Scheherazade comparison row?
Do root, triangle-seven, minus-seven, dominant extensions, accidentals, and slash basses read as one family?
Are triangle-seven and minus-seven compact but legible on mobile?
Do flat-nine and sharp-nine remain raised while root and bass accidentals remain inline?
Do dense mobile rows remain collision-free and fit the available pane width?
must_not_regress: staff engraving and non-chord SVG text

visual_constraints: Use only Scheherazade New Bold for document chord alphanumeric content and accidentals.
Use the approved 72% native-baseline quality suffix treatment.
Do not retain Edwin or JazzCord in the rendered document chord typography.
Keep root and slash-bass accidentals inline rather than optically separated into smaller tspans.
Avoid horizontal overflow and same-row collisions at all verified widths.
